zotero.abstractNote: Implicit learning, where models learn to perform tasks without explicit supervision, has become a cornerstone of modern machine learning. However, most existing methods are tailored to specific architectures or problem domains. In this work, we introduce Universal Algorithm-Implicit Learning (UAIL), a framework that enables models to learn algorithms implicitly across diverse tasks and architectures. By leveraging a novel meta-learning objective, UAIL allows models to adapt to new tasks by inferring the underlying algorithmic structure from data. We demonstrate the effectiveness of UAIL on a range of benchmarks, showing that it consistently outperforms existing implicit learning approaches and generalizes well to unseen tasks.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the International Conference on Machine Learning
